What this is

A preview of a duplicate-ready Notion client tracker organized around the public workflow visible on the site.

Public workflow used

a one-hour consultation so you have a chance to meet us, ask question, monthly visits until 30 weeks. Bi-weekly visits from 32-36 weeks, then weekly, follow-up care for all families at 24 hours, on day three/four, We work closely with Jennifer Vracin at Roots Billing

Today at Pine & Prairie Prenatal + birth + postpartum board Giselle K. Record Consult + Intake View Prenatal Cadence + On-Call Window Postpartum Visit + Newborn Care View Setup Map

Why this shape

From overview to usable workspace

The first screenshots show the main views. The later screenshots show the editable structure that would be delivered: fields, focused tables, individual client pages, and starter records.

What it helps with

  • One board replaces two cell phones + paper chart — consult through 6-week postpartum in one view
  • Prenatal cadence (monthly → bi-weekly → weekly) and on-call window (37-42 wks) tracked per client, not per calendar app
  • Homebirth logistics (birth kit, birth team, required doula for first-time moms, Roots Billing status) live with the client record

1. Today at Pine & Prairie

Today at Pine & Prairie

Active clients, who's in the 37-42 on-call window, today's prenatals and postpartum visits, and the day's consult — one snapshot replacing two cell phones and a paper chart.

Open this view

2. Prenatal + birth + postpartum board

Prenatal + birth + postpartum board

Every client moves left-to-right: Consult → Monthly prenatal → Bi-weekly → Weekly → On-call → Birth → Postpartum cadence → 6-wk close. First-time-mom doula requirement and Roots Billing status live on each card.

Open this view

3. Giselle K. Record

Giselle K. Record

Per-family page: EDD, gestational age, consult + intake, prenatal cadence, labs, homebirth logistics (birth kit, backup plan, waterbirth), birth team, and Roots Billing status — everything you used to assemble from the paper chart at each visit.

Open this view

4. Consult + Intake View

Consult + Intake View

The one-hour free consult in one screen: schedule, referral source, gestational age at consult, medical-history intake, records transfer, insurance verification with Roots Billing, and the first monthly prenatal already booked.

Open this view

5. Prenatal Cadence + On-Call Window

Prenatal Cadence + On-Call Window

This week's prenatals across the monthly → bi-weekly → weekly cadence, the current 37-42 wk on-call window with birth kits and birth teams confirmed, lab checkpoints (GBS, glucose), and Ellen's coverage lane.

Open this view

6. Postpartum Visit + Newborn Care View

Postpartum Visit + Newborn Care View

After every birth: 24h / day 3-4 / 1-wk / 2-wk / 6-wk cadence tracked per family with newborn weight, PKU/metabolic screen, lactation notes, and perineal healing — then a 6-wk close-out that hands Roots Billing the Global Maternity Code.

Open this view

7. Setup Map

Setup Map

Six-step setup: duplicate the template, move the intake + medical-history fields into the per-client record, import current clients into the cadence board, set visit reminders (GBS, glucose, postpartum), add a Roots Billing status field, and keep an on-call rotation lane for Allegra and Ellen.

Open this view
